title: Silky Nostalgic Filipino Ham Stir-fry
description: A floral and silky low-carb-friendly dish featuring ham with a touch of nostalgia suitable for breakfast or light meals.

ingredients.name: basil leaves
ingredients.notes: fresh is preferred but dried can be used

instructions: Preheat your oven to 350°F (175°C) and lightly season the ham pieces with salt and black pepper.
Place the seasoned ham on a baking sheet, bake for about 12-15 minutes until golden brown and crispy. Remove from oven and let cool slightly.
Heat up some oil in a non-stick pan over medium heat. Add finely chopped onion and sauté for about 3 minutes or until translucent.
Add the grated ginger to the pan and stir-fry for another minute, allowing it to release its aroma and soften.
Add cherry tomatoes (halved), green bell pepper pieces (if using whole peppers, chop them into small cubes), and red pepper flakes. Stir well and cook for 2-3 minutes until slightly softened.
Push the tomato-pepper mixture towards one side of the pan, making space at the other end. Add cooled ham pieces on top of the vegetables. Cook for about a minute to meld flavors together.
Sprinkle fresh basil leaves over the top and gently fold them into the dish. Let everything cook in the pan for another 1-2 minutes until all ingredients are heated through.
Remove from heat immediately after adding the basil leaves, as they can wilt easily with prolonged cooking time.
